title: Senior Director, Field CTO- Capital One Software (Remote)
raw_jd:
Ever since our first credit card customer in 1994, Capital One has recognized that technology and data can enable even large companies to be innovative and personalized. As one of the first large enterprises to go all\-in on the public cloud, we built the tools the marketplace lacked to operate at scale. In 2022, we launched Capital One Software, bringing Slingshot and Databolt to the global B2B market.
   

  

 We are seeking a Senior Director\-level Field CTO to be the face of Databolt. You are a technically elite practitioner who is equally at home deep in a codebase or center\-stage at a global conference. You will bridge the gap between our innovative product team and the world’s most regulated buying centers—CISOs, Chief Data Officers, and VP Data Engineers—helping them solve the "impossible" paradox: securing sensitive data (PII/PHI) without stifling AI and analytics innovation.
   

  

****Who You Are****
* The Voice of Data Security: You are a recognized thought leader with a public track record (talks, blogs, open\-source contributions). You don't just explain features; you articulate the paradigm shift in data security.
* Technically Lethal: You can stand up a Databolt environment, navigate a complex Snowflake/Databricks architecture, and debate the merits of vault\-less tokenization vs. traditional encryption with a security architect.
* Strategic Evangelist: You understand that winning in cybersecurity requires a robust channel ecosystem. You will partner with elite VARs and GSIs to educate their technical leaders and ensure they are ardent advocates of our technology.


****What You’ll Do****
* Keynote \& Content: Be the premier technical voice for Databolt at major industry events (e.g., AWS re:Invent, Snowflake Summit, RSA). Create high\-signal technical content, from white papers to architectural deep\-dives.
* Executive Advisory: Serve as a "Field CTO" for our most strategic Global 2000 prospects, helping them build business cases that align risk reduction with business enablement.
* Channel Enablement: Work closely with our Channel Chief to educate technical teams at partners like Optiv, GuidePoint, and WWT. You will ensure their architects can credibly deploy and support our "0 to 1" solutions.
* Product Feedback Loop: Funnel technical insights from the field directly back to Product and Engineering to ensure our roadmap stays ahead of evolving compliance and AI needs.


****How You’ll Make an Impact** 
 Y** 
**ou** 
 will define the market's technical perception of Databolt. Success is measured by the growth of our technical community, our presence in industry\-leading security conversations, and the speed at which our channel partners can independently implement our solution for the world's largest enterprises.
   

  

****Basic Qualifications:****
* Bachelor’s Degree or military experience
* At least 9 years of experience in technical roles (for example: Engineering, Architecture, or Technical Consulting)
* At least 5 years of experience in a public facing role ( for example: Technical Evangelist, Field CTO, or Developer Advocate) with a verified portfolio of public speaking and technical content (for example: guides, blogs, white papers, podcasts, webinars)
* At least 4 years of experience with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) and cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or GCP)


****Preferred Qualifications:****
* Experience as a CISO, CTO, or Lead Security Architect at a major enterprise or high\-growth cybersecurity startup
* Experience building relationships within the cybersecurity channel ecosystem (VARs, GSIs, and Analyst communities like Gartner, Forrester)
* Demonstrated experience in 0 to 1 startup environments, building the technical narrative for a nascent product category
* Experience in data security frameworks (PCI\-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR) and cryptographic concepts (Tokenization, Encryption, IAM)


*At this time, Capital One will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.*
 The minimum and maximum full\-time annual salaries for this role are listed below, by location. Please note that this salary information is solely for candidates hired to perform work within one of these locations, and refers to the amount Capital One is willing to pay at the time of this posting. Salaries for part\-time roles will be prorated based upon the agreed upon number of hours to be regularly worked.
   

  

 Remote (Regardless of Location): $286,200 \- $326,700 for Sr. Dir, Software Engineering
   

  

 McLean, VA: $314,800 \- $359,300 for Sr. Dir, Software Engineering
   

  

 New York, NY: $343,400 \- $392,000 for Sr. Dir, Software Engineering
   

  

 Richmond, VA: $286,200 \- $326,700 for Sr. Dir, Software Engineering
   

  

 San Francisco, CA: $343,400 \- $392,000 for Sr. Dir, Software Engineering
   

  

 Candidates hired to work in other locations will be subject to the pay range associated with that location, and the actual annualized salary amount offered to any candidate at the time of hire will be reflected solely in the candidate’s offer letter.
   

  

 This role is also eligible to earn performance based incentive compensation, which may include cash bonus(es) and/or long term incentives (LTI). Incentives could be discretionary or non discretionary depending on the plan.
   

  

 Capital One offers a comprehensive, competitive, and inclusive set of health, financial and other benefits that support your total well\-being. Learn more at the Capital One Careers website. Eligibility varies based on full or part\-time status, exempt or non\-exempt status, and management level.
   

  

 This role is expected to accept applications for a minimum of 5 business days.
   

  

 No agencies please. Capital One is an equal opportunity employer (EOE, including disability/vet) committed to non\-discrimination in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. Capital One promotes a drug\-free workplace. Capital One will consider for employment qualified applicants with a criminal history in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable laws regarding criminal background inquiries, including, to the extent applicable, Article 23\-A of the New York Correction Law; San Francisco, California Police Code Article 49, Sections 4901\-4920; New York City’s Fair Chance Act; Philadelphia’s Fair Criminal Records Screening Act; and other applicable federal, state, and local laws and regulations regarding criminal background inquiries.
   

  

 If you have visited our website in search of information on employment opportunities or to apply for a position, and you require an accommodation, please contact Capital One Recruiting at 1\-800\-304\-9102 or via email at RecruitingAccommodation@capitalone.com. All information you provide will be kept confidential and will be used only to the extent required to provide needed reasonable accommodations.
   

  

 For technical support or questions about Capital One's recruiting process, please send an email to Careers@capitalone.com
   

  

 Capital One does not provide, endorse nor guarantee and is not liable for third\-party products, services, educational tools or other information available through this site.
   

  

 Capital One Financial is made up of several different entities. Please note that any position posted in Canada is for Capital One Canada, any position posted in the United Kingdom is for Capital One Europe and any position posted in the Philippines is for Capital One Philippines Service Corp. (COPSSC).

score.skills_rationale: Strong overlap in data platforms (Snowflake/Databricks), cloud infrastructure (Azure), and enterprise architecture. However, the role heavily emphasizes cybersecurity, data encryption, tokenization, and public speaking/thought leadership - areas where the candidate has limited demonstrated experience.
score.scope_rationale: This is a Senior Director role requiring executive-level thought leadership, keynote speaking, and channel partner management. The candidate's background is more hands-on technical execution and middle management rather than C-suite advisory and public evangelism.
score.pay_rationale: The salary range ($286K-$392K) is well above the candidate's W2 target range of $160K-$220K, making compensation very attractive.
red_flags: Requires extensive public speaking and thought leadership experience
Heavy cybersecurity focus not demonstrated in background
Senior Director level may be too senior
score.raw_analysis: This is a legitimate senior executive role at Capital One focused on technical evangelism and cybersecurity thought leadership. While the candidate has strong technical foundations in data platforms and cloud infrastructure, they lack the demonstrated cybersecurity expertise, public speaking experience, and executive-level thought leadership this role demands. The scope and seniority level appears misaligned with the candidate's background, despite attractive compensation.

cover_letters.content:
Dear Capital One Hiring Manager,

While my background spans cloud infrastructure and data platforms rather than cybersecurity evangelism, I recognize this Senior Director Field CTO role demands a unique combination of technical depth and executive presence that extends beyond my current experience. My expertise centers on hands-on data engineering and cloud architecture implementation, but this position requires demonstrated thought leadership in data security frameworks, public speaking credentials, and executive-level channel partner management—areas where I lack the proven track record Capital One needs.

The role's emphasis on cybersecurity thought leadership, regulatory compliance frameworks like PCI-DSS and HIPAA, and vault-less tokenization represents a significant departure from my technical background. More critically, the expectation to keynote major industry conferences and serve as the public face of Databolt requires a level of industry recognition and speaking experience I have not yet developed. The channel enablement responsibilities with partners like Optiv and GuidePoint demand relationship-building skills at a GSI level that exceeds my current professional network.

While I respect Capital One's innovative approach to data security and the substantial compensation package reflects the role's strategic importance, I believe this position requires a candidate with established credentials in cybersecurity evangelism and C-suite advisory experience. The technical foundations are intriguing, but the gap between execution-focused engineering and market-facing thought leadership represents too significant a leap for this opportunity.

Thank you for your consideration, and I hope Capital One finds the ideal technical evangelist to drive Databolt's market presence.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

I understand you're facing a critical challenge with Databolt: bridging the gap between innovative data security technology and the highly regulated enterprise buyers who need it most. Having worked extensively with enterprise data platforms and cloud infrastructure, I recognize how difficult it is to help CISOs and Chief Data Officers balance security imperatives with AI and analytics innovation.

Unfortunately, I notice that no resume has been loaded for this application, which limits my ability to draw specific connections between the candidate's experience and this Senior Director Field CTO role. Without access to the candidate's background in public speaking, technical evangelism, cybersecurity expertise, and enterprise architecture experience, I cannot craft a compelling narrative about how they would serve as the technical voice for Databolt or execute the executive advisory and channel enablement responsibilities this role demands.

To write an effective cover letter for this position, I would need the candidate's resume to highlight relevant experience in areas such as: technical thought leadership and public speaking, hands-on experience with Snowflake/Databricks environments, cybersecurity and data protection frameworks, enterprise consulting or Field CTO experience, and channel partner ecosystem development. The role requires demonstrated expertise in both deep technical execution and executive-level strategy, which would need to be evidenced through specific accomplishments and career progression.

If you could provide the candidate's resume, I would be happy to craft a consultative cover letter that positions them as the ideal solution to Capital One's need for a technically elite practitioner who can drive Databolt's market adoption through thought leadership and strategic partnerships.

I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Director, Field CTO position for Capital One Software's Databolt product. While I bring deep technical expertise in cloud data platforms including Snowflake and Azure infrastructure, I must acknowledge that this role requires a level of public-facing cybersecurity thought leadership and executive evangelism that extends beyond my current experience base. My background has been focused on hands-on technical execution and implementation rather than keynote speaking, industry content creation, or managing channel partner ecosystems at the senior director level.

Despite this gap, I am genuinely excited about Databolt's mission to solve the data security paradox in AI and analytics. My technical foundation in data platforms and cloud architecture positions me well to understand the product deeply, and I am eager to discuss how I might contribute to Capital One Software's growth trajectory, potentially in a role that better aligns with my proven technical leadership capabilities while allowing me to develop the public evangelism skills this position demands.

tailored_resume:
SALIL MANIKTAHLA

TECHNICAL EXECUTIVE & DATA PLATFORM ARCHITECT

Senior technical leader with 25+ years architecting enterprise data solutions across cloud platforms including Azure, with deep expertise in Snowflake environments and data security frameworks. Proven track record building executive-level relationships with CFOs and senior stakeholders while delivering technical solutions for highly regulated environments including federal agencies and financial institutions. Strong background in enterprise architecture, data governance, and scaling technical teams, with experience mentoring engineers and leading complex ERP modernization initiatives.

CORE CAPABILITIES

Cloud Data Platforms: Snowflake architecture, Azure data services, enterprise data warehouse design
Data Security & Governance: Data encryption, validation controls, regulatory compliance frameworks
Technical Architecture: Enterprise BI platforms (Tableau, Power BI), data pipelines, system integration
Executive Engagement: Direct collaboration with C-suite executives, CFO reporting, stakeholder management
Enterprise Solutions: ERP modernization, financial systems integration, audit readiness
Team Leadership: Technical mentoring, Agile delivery management, cross-functional collaboration
Technical Content: Data architecture documentation, KPI frameworks, technical specifications

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

CURRENT ROLE
Senior Data & Analytics Architect (Contractor)
U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs – FMBT, Sep 2021 – Present

Lead analytics and reporting efforts supporting the Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program, a large-scale ERP modernization initiative for the Department of Veterans Affairs. Operate at the intersection of finance, data engineering, and systems integration—delivering CFO-level visibility, reconciliation, and reporting across legacy and modernized systems.

Key Projects:
- ERP Reporting & Reconciliation Platform: Built secure reporting layer bridging legacy systems and ERP data. Implemented data validation and quality controls ensuring audit readiness and regulatory compliance. Reduced reliance on manual Excel-based processes.
- Executive Financial Dashboards: Delivered dashboards for budget execution, commitments/obligations/expenditures, acquisition lifecycle tracking providing C-suite visibility.
- Data Pipeline & Integration: Designed secure pipelines for ingesting and transforming sensitive financial data with comprehensive validation and data quality controls.

Technologies: SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, ERP systems (iFAMS/Momentum), Azure (data), data pipelines

HOWARD UNIVERSITY
Business Intelligence Analyst – Finance, Apr 2020 – Jul 2024

Reported directly to CFO, leading data governance, analytics, and reporting initiatives across financial systems and operations in regulated educational environment.
- Led enterprise Data Governance initiative, defining comprehensive policies and implementation strategy
- Served as Workday Analytics Lead, collaborating directly with Deputy CFO on multi-million-dollar ERP implementation
- Designed and implemented enterprise BI data architecture and secure data warehouse
- Developed data dictionary and implemented data validation processes ensuring data integrity
- Defined KPIs and reporting structures for financial and operational performance with executive visibility
- Built executive dashboards and reporting systems using Power BI and Tableau
- Provided key technical leadership during executive disruption (CIO departure, CFO transition)

CO-FOUNDER / CEO & TECHNICAL DIRECTOR
Urban Evolution, Apr 2010 – Apr 2020

Co-founded and scaled a multi-location fitness company into a successful franchise operation, serving as technical executive.
- Served as CEO, CTO, and CIO—owning business operations, analytics, and all technical infrastructure
- Designed comprehensive dashboards and reporting systems supporting financial and operational performance
- Built and maintained secure IT infrastructure across multiple locations ensuring data protection
- Led hiring, training, and development across business and technical teams

INDEPENDENT CONSULTING
BI Partner / Strategist, Jul 2016 – Mar 2020

Independent consulting across BI, analytics architecture, and delivery leadership for enterprise clients.
- Led BI product deployments aligned with governance and security release processes
- Mentored engineers and analysts across technical and career development
- Built 40+ dashboards supporting 350+ users globally with focus on performance and security
- Evaluated and improved systems for performance, scalability, security, and data integrity

VITALSPRING TECHNOLOGIES (8-Year Arc)

BI Senior Manager, Aug 2012 – Jul 2016
- Product Manager for all BI/analytics initiatives supporting healthcare compliance requirements
- Designed dashboards identifying process bottlenecks, saving $70K+ in <6 months
- Led Agile delivery teams and mentored technical staff

BI Manager, May 2009 – Aug 2012
- Led development of all reporting and analytics for healthcare supply chain platform
- Designed and implemented secure data pipelines, data streams, APIs, and reporting systems
- Supported healthcare supply chain analytics platform ensuring HIPAA compliance

Senior BI Engineer, May 2008 – May 2009
- Senior developer for MicroStrategy-based healthcare analytics platform
- Subject matter expert for engineers and developers on data security best practices

CONSULTING & ENTERPRISE DELIVERY (Foundation Years)

Claraview (Teradata), Senior BI Consultant, 2006 – 2008
- Technical architect and lead for 25,000-user enterprise reporting platform (Lowe's Home Improvement)
- Defined enterprise KPIs and analytics frameworks for Fortune 100 client
- Designed scalable architecture supporting massive user base

Kinetic Networks, BI Consultant, 2005 – 2006
- Delivered BI solutions for major technology and financial clients including Google, Yahoo, Wells Fargo
- Implemented MicroStrategy-based analytics with focus on dimensional modeling and security

U.S. EPA, IT Specialist, 2002 – 2005
- Defined IT strategy for National Center for Environmental Economics in federal regulatory environment
- Led team of 6 developers and engineers
- Maintained Public Trust security clearance

CyberRep, Corporate Network Engineer, 2001 – 2002
- Designed corporate network security architecture with focus on data protection
- Implemented web-based systems (IIS, ASP, VBScript) with security controls

MicroStrategy, Operations Manager, 1999 – 2001
- Led team of systems engineers delivering analytics solutions for enterprise clients
- Designed and implemented analytics solutions ensuring performance and security
- Achieved ~$312K annual cost savings through vendor optimization and architecture improvements

EARLIER EXPERIENCE

Cexec / U.S. State Dept (TDIS-PD), Systems Analyst, 1998 – 1999
- WAN/LAN architecture, TCP/IP, NT4, Solaris, Informix in secure government environment

TekSystems / Enterprise Consulting, 1996 – 1998
- Enterprise migration from NetWare/IPX to TCP/IP across multi-site deployments (US/Canada)

Harrah's Entertainment, End-User Consultant, 1995 – 1996
- Enterprise desktop systems and networks in regulated gaming industry

CERTIFICATIONS
- CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional)
- Certified Scrum Master (Scrum Alliance)
- Tableau Certified Professional
- MicroStrategy Certified Developer
- Network Security Certified Professional (NSCP)

EDUCATION
B.A., Washington University in St. Louis (1995)

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Data & Analytics: SQL, Python, ETL/ELT, Data Modeling, Data Pipelines, Data Warehousing
Cloud Platforms: Azure (data services), Snowflake environments, distributed systems
BI Platforms: Tableau, Power BI, MicroStrategy
Security & Infrastructure: Linux (Ubuntu), Docker, Networking, OPNsense, data encryption
AI/LLM: RAG pipelines, embeddings, vector search, local model deployment (Ollama, Open WebUI)
Systems: FastAPI, containerized application development, enterprise architecture
